test: Phase 6 Test C5: Cluster dynamics vs lensing mass

case_A.label: mu-only dynamics (Sigma=1)
case_A.description: mu modifies Poisson equation only; lensing probes unmodified Weyl potential

case_B.label: Equal coupling (Sigma=mu, eta=1)
case_B.description: mu modifies both potentials equally; no gravitational slip

case_C.label: Parameterized slip
case_C.description: M_dyn/M_lens = mu/Sigma = 2/(1+1/eta) for varying gravitational slip eta

detectability.ref: Merten et al. 2015; Biviano et al. 2013

detectability.note: Hydrostatic mass bias, not directly M_dyn/M_lens

cluster_data.name: CLASH (WL+dynamics)
cluster_data.ref: Merten et al. 2015; Biviano et al. 2013

cluster_data.name: PSZ2 stacked (Planck SZ)
cluster_data.note: Hydrostatic mass bias, not directly M_dyn/M_lens

conclusion.key_unknown: gravitational slip eta (requires MTDF perturbation theory)
conclusion.summary: Case A (mu-only): 5.3% offset at z=0, marginally detectable with best stacked analyses. Case B (equal coupling): no offset, indistinguishable from GR. The actual prediction depends on MTDF's gravitational slip, which requires solving the perturbed field equations.
